text.title: AI Dockerfile Redflag List Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of AI Dockerfile Redflag List enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each step in the AI Dockerfile Redflag List playbook names the input artefact, the owner, the exit criterion, and where the output lands.
rule.rationale: Steps without exit criteria stay open. Steps without owners get done by no one or by everyone simultaneously.

rule.statement: Decision branches are explicit: 'if X then path A, else path B' with named signals, not vibes.

rationale.source: process design / Hammer & Champy, Reengineering the Corporation (1993)

rule.rationale: Implicit branches diverge by operator. Explicit branches converge.

rule.statement: Deviations from the playbook are logged with one-line rationale and surfaced in the next retro.

rationale.source: PDCA / Deming, Out of the Crisis (1986)

rule.rationale: Undocumented deviations destroy institutional memory. Logging makes the playbook self-improving.

rule.statement: Playbook ownership is named at the top — a single person who can authorize amendment.
rule.rationale: Orphaned playbooks rot. Named owners maintain.

example.title: Step with named owner and exit criterion
example.code: Step 2: Draft discovery findings Owner: BA on engagement Exit criterion: ≥5 stakeholder quotes, scored severity, signoff by client lead
example.note: Owner, output, and exit are explicit. No ambiguity.

example.title: Vague step
example.note: Different operators interpret 'capture' as transcript / summary / signed memo. Downstream consumer gets the wrong shape.
